dr_section_ref: DR §1 — Indonesian discourse particles (also as English loanword)
notes: RARE USAGE CAVEAT: 'also' as an Indonesian loanword is uncommon in casual speech — most speakers use 'juga'. This fixture exists because the original Story 3.2 AC explicitly enumerated 'also' as a TQ-1 rule. Code-switching 'Saya also lapar' = 'I'm also hungry' would more typically appear in younger urban speech mixing English vocabulary. VERIFY WITH GIRLFRIEND: does anyone in your conversation circle actually use 'also' this way, or should this rule be removed entirely? If never used, deleting the rule + fixtures simplifies maintenance.
